stages.name: targeted_unit_tests
stages.evidence: dogfood_production_test proves repeated production dogfood runs are deterministic (run1/run2 differ only in run id, timestamps, and durations and normalize to identical bytes -- closes F-025), the production report has no invalid mutants and stays in src/ production scope, the production dogfood config selects src modules in single-mode Debug, and scripts/ci.sh runs the required deterministic stages (format_check, build, unit_tests, task_system_validation, advisory_dogfood) network-independently with a --list mode; full suite green

stages.name: dogfood_smoke
stages.evidence: scripts/ci.sh --list enumerates the five deterministic stages in order; the advisory dogfood stage reviews survivors rather than failing; selected production-source dogfood is opt-in via scripts/dogfood-production.sh over test/fixtures/dogfood/production/config.toml

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 059 wires the initial advisory dogfood CI and is explicitly not the final release dogfood gate; full self-mutation dogfood gating is owned by task 085 after the late hardening tasks (061, 062, 064, 065, 066, 067) complete

residual_risk: The committed production dogfood reports (run1/run2) are deterministic reference fixtures used for the repeated-comparison test; a live run over real src modules via scripts/dogfood-production.sh is opt-in (kept out of the default fast, network-independent CI path) and its survivors are advisory until the final gate (task 085).

zentinel.dogfood.toml stays the fixture (stage 1) config -- it is pinned by the out-of-scope test/dogfood_fixture_test.zig -- so the selected production-source config lives at test/fixtures/dogfood/production/config.toml.

scripts/ci.sh runs the advisory dogfood via scripts/dogfood.sh (fixture) by default to keep CI fast and deterministic; expanding the default advisory pass to the production runner is a later CI-integration step (task 064).
